raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California is spending approximately $250 million to subsidize empty homes to the primary benefit of wealthier individuals.",
    "The homeownership rate for Californians is the second lowest in the nation at 53.8%.",
    "Homeownership rates are 34.5% for Black people and 41.9% for Latinos in California.",
    "California's history of redlining has had lasting impacts on generational wealth-building through homeownership.",
    "State mortgage deductions similar to California's are regressive and inefficient, resulting in less-affordable homes.",
    "Mortgage deductions increase the likelihood of mortgage defaults and reduce the homeownership rate by about 5%.",
    "The mortgage interest deduction is not effective at serving first-time homebuyers.",
    "Approximately less than 2.8% of Californians currently write off mortgage interest on vacation homes.",
    "If the mortgage interest deduction for second homes is discontinued, the wealthier-than-average Californian would owe about $1,000 in taxes annually.",
    "The $250 million could be used to create approximately 23,000 first-time homebuyers."
  ]
}
```
